head.title: Somnath Dwarka Tour From Hyderabad, Flights Included
head.meta_description: Hyderabad’s Rajkot non-stop runs 3 days a week but lands late morning, so catch it and reach Dwarka by evening. Flights in. Ground from Rs.15,000 pp.
head.h1: Somnath Dwarka Tour Package From Hyderabad, With Flights Included

copy.quick_answer: A Somnath Dwarka tour package from Hyderabad turns on two facts about one flight. Hyderabad’s only non-stop into the circuit is an IndiGo service to Rajkot Hirasar that runs three days a week, and the morning one departs about 09:25 and lands about 11:20. That morning arrival is the good part: it is early enough to drive to Dwarka and reach the evening darshan the same day, so we run the circuit the normal way, Dwarka first, and your first day is a real one rather than a travel day. The ground portion is fixed at Rs. 5,000 per person per night, so four days is Rs. 15,000 per person at 3 star, and with the return flight the all-in is typically around Rs. 27,000 per person, quoted for your dates. The one thing to sort first is the date, because the flight runs only three days a week.

sections.h2: Three flights a week, and why the date comes first
body: Hyderabad has one non-stop into this circuit, an IndiGo service to Rajkot Hirasar, and it runs three days a week, not daily. So unlike a city with hourly options, a Hyderabad trip starts by matching your dates to one of those three flights.

Airline pages will show you ten daily Hyderabad to Jamnagar flights, which sound better because Jamnagar is nearer Dwarka. They are connections, not non-stops, and they turn a short trip into a long one. The real non-stop lands at Rajkot.
Get the date onto one of the three weekly Rajkot flights and the rest of the trip is the easiest of any fly-in, because of when it lands.

sections.h2: The morning arrival that gives you a full first day
body: The morning Hyderabad to Rajkot flight departs about 09:25 and lands about 11:20, under two hours. Hirasar is about 30 km outside Rajkot.

An 11:20 landing changes everything about day one. It is early enough to drive the 225 km to Dwarka, about four and a half hours, and reach the temple around 16:30, just as it reopens for the evening at 5:00 pm. So your first day ends with a Dwarkadhish darshan, not a hotel check-in after dark.

That is why Hyderabad runs the circuit Dwarka first, the normal direction, while the afternoon-arriving Bangalore flight has to run Somnath first. The morning slot is the whole advantage, and it is worth planning your dates around.
We build day one around your actual flight, and if only the evening service runs on your dates we tell you, because that one lands too late for a same-day Dwarka darshan and changes the plan.

sections.h2: If your dates miss the three weekly flights
body: If none of the three weekly Rajkot non-stops fits your dates, there are two honest fallbacks.
One is to connect. Hyderabad to Jamnagar or Rajkot via Ahmedabad or Mumbai runs on many more days, at the cost of a stop and a longer travel day.
The other is the non-stop to Ahmedabad, which is frequent, and then the drive. Ahmedabad is 440 km from Dwarka, 8 to 9 hours, so it is the long way in but it keeps you on a single flight.
We lay out which of the three weekly Rajkot flights is closest to your dates against these fallbacks, and let you choose with the tradeoffs in front of you.

sections.h2: What all inclusive means here, and what it costs
body: This is one of the package classes with the flight in the price. We book the return flight, the hotels, the breakfast and the vehicle with driver for the full circuit.

The ground portion is fixed. Four days and three nights is Rs. 15,000 per person at the 3 star tier, Rs. 30,000 at 4 star and Rs. 37,500 at 5 star, GST included, with hotel, breakfast, vehicle, tolls, parking, fuel and the driver’s food and stay all inside it.

The flight is the part that moves. Hyderabad to Rajkot non-stop has started around Rs. 6,004 each way and changes with the date and the booking window, so a four day all-in at 3 star typically lands around Rs. 27,000 per person. We quote the exact combined figure for your dates.

Because the non-stop is three a week, booking early matters more here than on a daily route, both to hold the fare and to get a seat on the day that suits you. On Janmashtami and Diwali both halves rise, the ground rate up to threefold, so those dates are booked about three months ahead.

days.title: Day 1: Hyderabad to Rajkot in the morning, then Dwarka by evening

stops.text: The morning IndiGo non-stop leaves Hyderabad for Rajkot Hirasar, under two hours.
Land at Hirasar. We meet you and set off for Dwarka, 225 km, about four and a half hours.
Reach Dwarka as the temple prepares to reopen, check in at your tier, and take the evening Dwarkadhish darshan after 5:00 pm.
Gomti Ghat and the Sudama Setu footbridge in the evening. A full first day, not a lost one.

days.title: Days 2 and 3: The middle of the trip, shared by every start city
days.intro: Once you are on the circuit your start city stops mattering, so rather than reprint the same two days on every origin page we hand them to the plan that owns them. Two full days of Dwarka, Bet Dwarka across Sudarshan Setu, Nageshwar and the coastal run to Somnath through Porbandar, set out hour by hour on the four day plan. The full hour by hour version of both days sits on the four day plan, and the drive-from-Rajkot detail is on the Rajkot page. The morning arrival lets Hyderabad run it in the normal Dwarka-first order. See /somnath-dwarka-tour-package/4-days-3-nights/

days.title: Day 4: Somnath morning, then the timed drive to the return flight

stops.text: Mangala Aarti and darshan at the Somnath Jyotirlinga, with Bhalka Tirth and the Ahalyabai temple nearby.
Drive back toward Rajkot Hirasar, 197 km, timed to the afternoon return flight.
Rajkot Hirasar for the non-stop back to Hyderabad, which runs on the same three days.
Land in Hyderabad the same evening.

price_sheet.intro: Two parts, one quote. The ground portion is fixed per person per night. The return flight is added at the live fare, and the combined figure is quoted for your dates.

table.hotels: The Fern Sattva Resort at Dwarka, best property in town at Somnath, named in your quote

price_sheet.all_in_note: All in note. The all-in figures assume a return Hyderabad to Rajkot non-stop from around Rs. 6,004 each way. Because the non-stop runs three a week, fares can be higher near those dates, so we quote the exact combined number for your dates rather than hold you to an estimate.
price_sheet.gst_note: Gst note. Every ground price here is final and includes GST. Nothing is added at checkout beyond the flight, which is shown to you at its actual fare.
price_sheet.group_and_child: Group and child. The ground rate is per person on twin sharing. In a group of about six the 3 star ground rate falls to around Rs. 4,000 per person per night on triple sharing, and to around Rs. 3,500 in a group of about twelve. A solo traveller pays the base plus Rs. 3,500 for the same services. Children under five travel free on the ground portion, and from five they are counted as an adult. Airfare is per seat at whatever the airline charges for the age.
price_sheet.vehicle_note: Vehicle note. Group size picks the vehicle. Sedan for 2 to 3, Ertiga for 4 to 5, Innova Crysta for 5 to 6, 9 seater Tempo Traveller for 7 to 9, 12 seater Maharaja or Urbania for 10 to 12, 26 seater Tempo Traveller above that.
price_sheet.included_note: Included note. The package covers the return Hyderabad flight, the hotel at your tier, breakfast, the vehicle with driver for the full circuit, and all road tolls, parking, fuel and the driver’s food and stay. You land to a car and a plan.
price_sheet.five_star_honesty: Five star honesty. No five star Taj or ITC property operates in Dwarka or in Somnath. In Dwarka the top tier puts you in The Fern Sattva Resort. At Somnath it buys the best room in town, named in your quote before you pay. Taj and ITC enter your plan only on the legs that reach them, which this circuit does not.
price_sheet.blackout_note: Blackout note. Two dates break this rate card. On Janmashtami and Diwali, ground demand can push prices up to three times these numbers and airfares climb with them, and we take a limited number of bookings made about three months ahead. If your travel lands on either, ask early and we will quote the real figure rather than a floor you cannot get.
price_sheet.exclusions: Lunch and dinner, personal pooja, seva and abhishek at either temple, the sound and light show ticket, camera and cloakroom charges, tips, in-flight meals, seat selection and excess baggage charged by the airline, and anything you buy yourself. The Gir safari permit is extra if you add Gir.
price_sheet.cancellation: Cancellation. We confirm a booking on 50 percent advance, and you pay the remaining 50 percent after you arrive, during the journey. The flight follows the airline’s own fare rules, which we tell you before you pay. Cancellation, refunds and the payment schedule for the ground portion are set out in full on our cancellation and refund policy, and the booking terms sit alongside it. Read both before you pay.
price_sheet.trust_warning: Trust warning. The Shree Somnath Trust accepts pooja and donation bookings only through its own website and warns that it never asks for payment through private numbers, WhatsApp or UPI. Book sevas yourself at somnath.org.

why_choose: We plan your dates onto one of the three weekly Rajkot non-stops first, because the flight, not the tour, is the scarce part.
We use the morning arrival the way it should be used: straight to Dwarka for the evening darshan, so day one is a full day.
We tell you the Hyderabad to Jamnagar ten-daily flights are connections, not the non-stop, rather than sell you the marketing.
One price, one booking. The return flight, the hotels, the breakfast and the car are all ours to arrange.
A local Gujarat team meets your flight and drives the circuit. One number, +91 7302265809, on call and WhatsApp.
We reconfirm the flight and every timing before departure, because a three-a-week flight leaves no room for a scheduling surprise.
copy.not_for_you_if_heading: This trip is not for you if
not_for_you_if: You need to travel on a fixed date that cannot flex. The good Rajkot non-stop runs three days a week, and if your date misses all three you are on a connection or the long Ahmedabad drive.
You want to land nearest Dwarka non-stop. Hyderabad’s ten-daily Jamnagar flights are connections, so the nearest airport to Dwarka is not a non-stop from Hyderabad.
You want one fixed all-in price months out regardless of the airfare. The ground half is fixed, the flight half moves, and an honest all-in is quoted per date.
You want to fly in and see both temples in a day. Even with a full first day at Dwarka, both temples need the four days.
You want a five star Taj in Dwarka. It does not exist, and anyone selling you one should be asked for the property name.

faq.q: How often does the Hyderabad to Rajkot flight run?
faq.a: The IndiGo non-stop to Rajkot Hirasar runs three days a week, with a morning service landing about 11:20 and an evening one. Because it is not daily, a Hyderabad trip starts by matching your dates to one of the three flights, and the morning one is the one to aim for.

faq.q: Is there a direct flight from Hyderabad to Dwarka or Jamnagar?
faq.a: There is no airport at Dwarka, and the ten-daily Hyderabad to Jamnagar flights that airlines market are connections, not non-stops. Hyderabad’s real non-stop into the circuit is to Rajkot Hirasar, about 225 km from Dwarka and 197 km from Somnath.

faq.q: How do I reach Dwarka from Hyderabad by flight?
faq.a: Fly the morning Hyderabad to Rajkot Hirasar non-stop, landing about 11:20, and we drive you straight to Dwarka for the evening darshan the same day. If your dates miss the three weekly flights, we route you via a connection or the cheaper Ahmedabad flight with a longer drive.

faq.q: How much is a Dwarka Somnath package from Hyderabad with flights?
faq.a: The ground portion is fixed at Rs. 15,000 per person for four days at 3 star, GST included. With a return Hyderabad to Rajkot flight from around Rs. 6,004 each way, the all-in is typically around Rs. 27,000 per person, and we quote the exact figure for your dates.

faq.q: How many days do you need for Dwarka and Somnath from Hyderabad?
faq.a: Four days and three nights. The morning arrival gives you a full first day at Dwarka, and both temples, the island, the bridge and the coast still need three nights. Because the flight is three a week, we plan the whole trip around the dates it runs.

final_cta.summary: The morning fly-in: Hyderabad’s Rajkot non-stop runs three days a week but lands before noon, so catch it and you reach Dwarka for the evening darshan the same day. Ground fixed at Rs. 15,000 per person at 3 star, all-in typically around Rs. 27,000, quoted for your dates. Send your dates first, because the flight is the scarce part.
